concerns.text: Plan treats correlation analysis between mathematical invariants as predictive modeling requiring 'exploratory validation sample' split. Hyperbolic volume relationships with crossing/braid indices are mathematical properties, not ML predictions. Validation sample split is conceptually misaligned with research question nature. Should reframe as full-dataset correlation analysis with confidence intervals, not train/validation split.

No power analysis or effective sample size quantification. Plan targets 9988 prime knots ≤13, but torus/satellite knots excluded (no hyperbolic volume). Actual N for regression analysis is unknown. Phase 1 validation scope limited to ≤10. Without effective N, cannot assess whether sample is sufficient to detect expected effect sizes for correlation/regression.

Composite complexity score constructed as 'weighted combination of crossing number and braid index' with acknowledged 'no established mathematical basis in knot theory literature'. Using this exploratory construct as validation target for correlation with hyperbolic volume creates circular reasoning: composite score is validated against hyperbolic volume, then used to claim predictive power. Should either (1) remove composite score validation or (2) clearly label as exploratory construct without predictive claims.

Hyperbolic volume selection bias acknowledged (torus/satellite knots excluded) but not quantified. This creates non-random sample of all prime knots. If relationship between crossing/braid indices and volume differs for excluded knots, generalizability is limited. Should quantify proportion of excluded knots and discuss impact on conclusions.
